data-refinement.title: Data refinement & hygiene — fix NULL descriptions, notes, types, edges, and chunk gaps across the entire graph
data-refinement.notes:
Phase 1 DONE: all node types migrated to 8-type system (0 NULLs), all content nodes have dates, podcast/paper-club/builders-club fully populated (desc + notes). 216 junk nodes deleted, duplicates merged.

Phase 2 remaining: ainews desc/notes (136), article desc/notes (60), guest desc/notes (732), entity desc/notes (2554), edge cleanup (7128 ai_similarity edges), UI date display fix.

Depends on: content-ingestion (DONE), schema-and-port (DONE)
Blocks: discord-bot-v2 (bot quality depends on clean data)

tasks.text: Phase 0: Audit + graph structure decision (flat types, no hubs)
Phase 1A: Node type migration — episode/source/person/org/topic → 8-type system (0 NULLs)
Phase 1A: Delete junk nodes — 58 AIE talks, 65 insights, 31 placeholders, 7 promo posts
Phase 1A: Merge duplicates — 52 source-podcast pairs, 2 paper-club pairs
Phase 1B: Publish dates — all 510 content nodes have event_date (yt-dlp + Substack scraping)
Phase 1C: Podcast descriptions + notes — 247/247 complete
Phase 1C: Paper-club descriptions + notes — 32/32 complete
Phase 1C: Builders-club descriptions + notes — 24/24 complete
Phase 2A: AINews descriptions + notes — 136 nodes, 0 done
Phase 2B: Article descriptions + notes — 71 nodes, 11 done, 60 remaining
Phase 2C: Guest descriptions + notes — 735 nodes, 3 done, 732 remaining
Phase 2D: Entity descriptions + notes — 2562 nodes, 8 done, 2554 remaining
Phase 2E: Edge cleanup — 7128 ai_similarity edges need audit + context rewrite
Phase 2F: UI date display fix — sort by event_date, show publish date instead of updated_at
Verify: confirm zero NULLs across all types, spot-check quality

dashboard-and-categories.title: Dashboard + Category Taxonomy — 8 canonical categories, dashboard overview, sidebar overhaul
dashboard-and-categories.notes:
Two things: (1) a dashboard view as the default landing page showing stats + 8 category cards with previews, and (2) replace generic node_type values with 8 branded categories (podcast, guest, article, entity, builders-club, paper-club, workshop, ainews). Updates sidebar to fixed category list with icons.

Depends on: data-refinement (type migration uses new taxonomy)
Blocks: discord-bot-v2 (bots need to understand the 8 categories)

tasks.text: Create src/config/categories.ts — canonical 8-category config with icons and sort modes
Update NodeType in src/types/database.ts to new 8 categories + hub
Update LeftTypePanel.tsx — fixed category list, icons, branded labels, fixed order
Build GET /api/dashboard endpoint — stats + category previews in single call
Build Dashboard.tsx component — stats bar + 8 category cards with preview items
Update MainViewSwitcher.tsx — add dashboard view, set as default
Update ThreePanelLayout.tsx — render dashboard, default activeView = dashboard
Update scripts/refine-data.ts --task types for new taxonomy — already done in PRD-10
Clean up metadata interfaces in database.ts
Type-check + build + verify

documentation-overhaul.title: Documentation overhaul — rewrite all docs for LS Hub identity, split internal + public-facing
documentation-overhaul.notes:
Current docs were copied from RA-H and don't reflect the product. Two layers: (1) internal developer docs covering architecture, categories, bots, MCP, ingestion, search, and contributing, and (2) public-facing guides accessible in the live app.

No blocking dependencies.

auto-ingestion.title: Auto-ingestion pipeline — hourly RSS/API discovery, graph ingest, and #yap bot trigger
auto-ingestion.notes: Hub-side foundation implemented: shared ingestion service, cron endpoints, ingestion run log table + admin API, and Discord webhook notifications with Sig mentions. Remaining work includes bot-repo changes for Slop auto-response and admin UI surface.

tasks.text: Create ingestion source config + discovery modules (YouTube RSS, Substack RSS, GitHub API/Atom fallback)
Create shared processing pipeline (extract → node → chunk/embed → entity edges)
Create ingestion_runs logging module with overlap guard and run summaries
Build GET /api/cron/ingest with auth + source/dry-run support
Build POST /api/cron/ingest/trigger for manual admin-triggered runs
Build GET /api/admin/ingestion-log for run history + health windows
Add hourly cron schedule in vercel.json
Add Discord #yap webhook notifications with Sig mention
Bot repo: add #yap to ALLOWED_CHANNEL_IDS and Slop auto-response to Sig in #yap
Dashboard UI: add ingestion section or /admin/ingestion page
Hardening: retry policy tuning, per-source failure alerts, and carry-over queue when nearing runtime budget

discord-bot-v2.title: Discord bot refinement — contextual grounding, content-type awareness, temporal intelligence, channel architecture
discord-bot-v2.notes:
Bots are live and working but need deeper grounding. They need to understand WHAT Latent Space is, navigate content by type and time, and operate in a proper channel architecture (bot-talk channel + user thread model).

Depends on: data-refinement (clean data = better retrieval), discord-bot v1 (DONE)
Blocks: LS Discord rollout

tasks.text: Rewrite start-here guide with full LS identity, content types, and temporal context
Write content-types guide covering podcast/article/newsletter/meetup/paper-club with query patterns
Add temporal awareness — bots understand dates, can answer 'recent', 'last month', 'timeline of X'
Update Sig persona — grounded in LS identity, cites with type+date context
Update Slop persona — grounded in LS identity, hot takes reference specific content
Implement bot-talk channel — dedicated channel where Sig & Slop engage each other on new material
Implement user thread model — user question creates thread, ongoing conversation lives in thread
Test end-to-end in test Discord server
Demo to swyx
Add to LS Discord
Stretch: Build daily audio recap pipeline (collate → script → TTS → mix → publish to YouTube)
Stretch: Publish first Sig vs Slop daily recap episode

storylines.title: Storylines / narrative arcs — temporal narrative layer connecting content, entities, and claims over time
storylines.notes:
BBC-inspired narrative layer. A storyline is a node_type='storyline' that tracks arcs like 'the scaling laws debate' or 'open vs closed source AI' across episodes, guests, and claims over time. Enables editorial planning and temporal bot queries.

Future — revisit after core graph is populated and bot is live.

Depends on: schema-and-port (DONE), content-ingestion (DONE), data-refinement

tasks.text: Define storyline metadata schema
Add storyline edge types (advances, central_to, subsumes)
Add temporal edge metadata (valid_from, valid_until)
Create initial storylines from existing content
Storyline timeline UI component
Bot can answer 'trace the arc of X across episodes'
Auto-detect emerging storylines from new content
